title: Chronic Followup Recall Orchestration
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: chronic followup recall orchestration.
assumptions: Time is discretized into atomic time_slot_candidate tokens; provisional_time_block represents coarser administrative reservations distinct from atomic slots.
Actions are atomic from the planner viewpoint: resource availability updates are immediate and consistent (no partial application).
holding_case and finalizing_case subtypes are available to disambiguate operations that otherwise would accept two recall_case-typed parameters.

Resource availability predicates are authoritative: when an action consumes an availability fact (e.g., clinician or slot), that resource is considered reserved until an explicit release action restores availability.
risks: Parameter-role confusion between case-typed parameters: actions that accept two recall_case-typed arguments risk mixing the roles of 'case being finalized' and 'case holding a reservation' (mitigation: use distinct subtypes T17/T18 and clear parameter naming in implementation).
Concurrent scheduling without transactional locking could permit double-booking if provisional blocks and slot claims are not synchronized across agents.
Resource exhaustion: planners may leave cases in partial states (e.g., reserved slot without clinician assignment) if downstream actions fail or are starved, requiring compensating clean-up.
Complex booking_pattern logic (T10) may be underspecified, leading to incorrect holds or invalid finalizations if patterns are ambiguous or overlap.
Human-in-the-loop steps (scheduling_administrator actions) create timing uncertainty; assumptions about immediacy may not hold in operational deployments.
